lines.en_lit: as it is written: in the wilderness” And the one said are prohibited as it is written, “cut off” [gezeira],

And the one said are prohibited, what does he do this wilderness, He needs it for that which was taught “Into the wilderness” “into the wilderness” “in the wilderness” to include Nov and Givon Shiloh, and the eternal Temple.
And the other, what does he do with this gezeira? He needs it for that which was taught gezeira: Gezeira nothing other cut. Alternatively, gezeira nothing other something that breaks apart and falls,
Alternatively, gezeira lest you say is a meaningless act, the verse states: “I am the Lord” I, the Lord, decreed it [gezartiv], and you have no right to question it.
Rava said: It is reasonable in accordance with the one that said are permitted, the Torah did not say: “And send to mishap.

The Sages taught: Azazel that should be rough and hard. might in a settled the verse states: “In the wilderness.” And from where that a cliff? The verse states “gezeira,” It was taught in another Azazel the hardest mountain, and so it says: “And the mighty [eilei] of the land he took away”
The school of Rabbi Yishmael taught: Azazel it atones for the actions of Uzza and Azael.

The Sages taught “You shall do My ordinances, matters that, had they not been written, logical that they be written. They are idol worship, prohibited sexual relations, bloodshed, theft, and blessing God,

And you shall keep my statutes, matters that Satan challenge They are: eating pork; wearing diverse kinds ḥalitza a yevama, the purification of the leper; and the scapegoat. And lest you say these are meaningless acts, the verse states: “I am the Lord” I am the Lord, I decreed these and you have no right to doubt them.

what point the garments rendered impure. The Sages taught The garments of the one who dispatches are rendered impure, but the garments of the one who dispatches the dispatcher, are not rendered impure.

might he leaves the wall of the courtyard the verse states: “He that dispatched” if He that dispatched, might until he reaches the cliff. the verse states: “And he that dispatched,” How is this when he emerged outside the wall of Jerusalem. the statement of Rabbi Yehuda.

Rabbi Yosei says Azazel shall wash” until he has reached the cliff. Rabbi Shimon says: “And he that dispatched the goat to Azazel shall wash his clothes” he throws it headlong his garments are rendered impure.
MISHNA: he came next to the bull and the goat that were to be burned. He tore their and removed the portions and placed them in a large bowl [magis] and burned them on the altar.

He interwove into braids took them out to the place burning, And at what point do the render garments impure, from that they emerged outside the wall of the courtyard. Rabbi Shimon says: from that the fire has ignited in most of
GEMARA: Would it enter your mind he burned them? Rather, say to burn them on the altar

interwove into braids Rabbi Yoḥanan said: like a braid, It was taught: He would not dissect dissection of the flesh of a burnt-offering, Rather, skin on top of the flesh From where are these matters As it was taught Rabbi says: It is stated here skin and flesh and dung and it is stated below, skin and flesh and dung
